description_long: This is a blue, single-propeller fighter airplane reminiscent of World War II-era designs, featuring a streamlined fuselage and elliptical wings with roundels and insignia typical of the Royal Air Force. The aircraft has a single-seat, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy positioned just behind the engine cowling. The wings are low-mounted and display prominent roundels, while the tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the landing gear is in a taildragger configuration, with two main wheels and a small tail wheel. The fuselage bears the code 'JMR' and additional insignia, adding to its historical military appearance. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal, with some glass for the canopy and rubber for the tires.
description: A blue, single-propeller WWII-style fighter airplane with elliptical low-mounted wings, RAF roundels, and an enclosed single-seat cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, nose, and symmetrical elliptical wings with roundels.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cockpit canopy, fuselage markings, and the taildragger landing gear.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, roundels, and the streamlined shape of the fuselage and tail.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other side of the fuselage, cockpit, and tail section.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight elliptical wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy just behind the engine cowling.
